refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level acceptance criterion that conflates productivity measurement with operational capability. Refinement separates the measurement/verification aspect from the capability delivery aspect, and explicitly acknowledges dual operator modes (on-site and tele-operated) as distinct execution contexts. This clarification is essential because productivity depends on both machine capability and operator effectiveness, which differ between direct and remote control.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trials under agreed conditions (soil type, task profile, weather, operator experience level). Measure actual output (m³/h or ton/h) and compare against productivity_target. Log operator mode (on-site vs. remote), task type, and environmental conditions. Verify compliance with safety plan via incident/near-miss logs and safety audit checklist.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The Vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with mechanical capability sufficient to achieve productivity_target under nominal operating conditions.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The on-site Operator shall be able to command and monitor the Vehicle with sufficient situational awareness and control responsiveness to sustain productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The Tele-operator shall be able to command and monitor the Vehicle via Teleoperation with sufficient latency, fidelity, and safety interlocks to sustain productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The Safety Services shall continuously monitor operational state and enforce safety constraints (worksite plan, policies) without degrading productivity below acceptable threshold.
FR-NGHE-001.5: The NGHE Solution shall provide real-time Telemetry to Site Manager and Maintenance for productivity tracking, task logging, and predictive maintenance scheduling.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate a high-power hydraulic earthmoving vehicle with on-board telematics, dual control interfaces (cab and remote), and real-time task/productivity logging; couple with safety monitoring middleware that enforces geofence and load-limit constraints.

DP-B: Deploy a modular vehicle platform with swappable attachments (bucket, blade, compactor) and adaptive control algorithms that optimize cycle time and fuel efficiency; integrate teleoperation gateway with latency compensation and operator skill-level adaptation.

DP-C: Implement a productivity-centric architecture: Vehicle executes core earthmoving tasks; Teleoperation module provides remote command/feedback with safety interlocks; Safety Services module enforces real-time constraints; Telemetry module streams performance data to Site Manager dashboard for continuous KPI tracking and predictive maintenance triggers.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C: Implement a productivity-centric architecture: Vehicle executes core earthmoving tasks; Teleoperation module provides remote command/feedback with safety interlocks; Safety Services module enforces real-time constraints; Telemetry module streams performance data to Site Manager dashboard for continuous KPI tracking and predictive maintenance triggers.

potential_sources: Teleoperation latency may couple with Safety Services constraint enforcement (e.g., emergency stop must override remote command within safety-critical time window).
Vehicle hydraulic/mechanical performance couples with Teleoperation control fidelity (e.g., bucket fill rate must be predictable for operator to maintain cycle time).
Telemetry data collection couples with real-time task execution (e.g., high-frequency logging may consume bandwidth or CPU, affecting responsiveness).
coupling_check.notes: DP-C is modular and separates concerns (Vehicle capability, Teleoperation interface, Safety enforcement, Telemetry reporting), reducing coupling. However, the three coupling sources identified above require explicit interface contracts and latency budgets at the next decomposition level. Recommended: define maximum acceptable latency for Teleoperation (e.g., <500ms round-trip), safety constraint response time (e.g., <100ms), and telemetry sampling rate (e.g., 10 Hz for non-critical metrics, 100 Hz for safety-critical signals).

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Operator skill level (on-site and remote) is adequate to sustain productivity_target; risk: inadequate training or fatigue may degrade performance below target regardless of system capability.

Assumption: Worksite conditions (soil type, slope, obstacles) are within design envelope; risk: extreme conditions (e.g., very soft clay, steep slopes) may reduce achievable productivity below target.
Assumption: Network connectivity for Teleoperation is reliable (e.g., 4G/5G or private LTE); risk: network outages or latency spikes may force fallback to on-site operation, reducing flexibility.
Assumption: Safety Services constraints do not conflict with productivity targets; risk: overly conservative safety limits (e.g., very low speed limits) may make productivity_target unachievable.

Assumption: Telemetry infrastructure (sensors, data pipeline, dashboard) is available and maintained; risk: sensor failures or data pipeline outages may blind Site Manager to productivity trends and maintenance needs.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-C is sound but should be refined to explicitly address the three coupling sources: (1) define Teleoperation latency budget and safety override protocol; (2) specify Vehicle control responsiveness requirements (e.g., bucket fill rate, boom speed) to support operator cycle-time targets; (3) establish Telemetry sampling strategy (adaptive sampling: high-frequency for safety-critical signals, lower frequency for trend data) to avoid bandwidth/CPU contention. Additionally, add a requirement for operator training and certification to mitigate the skill-level risk.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level integration module that orchestrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and Telemetry subsystems to deliver productivity-centric earthmoving capability. It owns the system-level interfaces (Tasking input from Site Manager, Telemetry output to Site Manager, SafetyAlert broadcast to all stakeholders) and enforces the overall productivity KPI acceptance criterion.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Site Manager or Tele-operator sends task commands (e.g., 'excavate zone A', 'load truck B', 'move to staging area') to NGHE Solution. Tasking includes task ID, location, target volume/tonnage, priority, and safety constraints (e.g., no operation near power lines).

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution streams real-time and historical telemetry to Site Manager dashboard and Maintenance system. Includes Vehicle state (position, orientation, attachment status), task progress (volume excavated, tonnage loaded), productivity metrics (m³/h, ton/h, cycle time), fuel consumption, and equipment health indicators (hydraulic pressure, temperature, wear estimates).

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution broadcasts safety alerts to all stakeholders (Site Manager, Operator, Tele-operator, Safety Manager) when safety constraints are violated or safety-critical events occur (e.g., geofence breach, load limit exceeded, emergency stop triggered, operator incapacity detected).

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution maintains network connectivity for Teleoperation command/feedback, Telemetry data upload, and SafetyAlert broadcast. Network Link is shared with Teleoperation module for remote control and with Telemetry module for data streaming.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level container for CR-NGHE-001 (Productivity KPI). It owns the system-level acceptance criterion, orchestrates all subsystems, and manages external interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link). Minimal diffusion: only the Network Link interface is shared with Teleoperation and Telemetry subsystems, which is acceptable as a cross-cutting concern.

Vehicle owns the mechanical/hydraulic capability to execute earthmoving tasks, which is necessary but not sufficient for CR-NGHE-001. Productivity also depends on Teleoperation (remote control capability), Safety Services (constraint enforcement), and Telemetry (performance tracking). Assigning CR-NGHE-001 to Vehicle alone would diffuse the productivity KPI across multiple subsystems and obscure system-level accountability.

Teleoperation owns the remote control interface and latency-critical command/feedback loop, which is necessary for tele-operated productivity but not for on-site operation. Assigning CR-NGHE-001 to Teleoperation alone would exclude on-site operation and create artificial coupling between productivity and remote control mode.

Safety Services owns constraint enforcement and safety monitoring, which is necessary to ensure productivity is achieved safely but not sufficient to deliver productivity itself. Assigning CR-NGHE-001 to Safety Services would invert the responsibility hierarchy (safety should enable productivity, not define it).

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: CR-NGHE-001 (Productivity KPI) is a system-level acceptance criterion that requires integration of Vehicle capability, Teleoperation interface, Safety Services constraints, and Telemetry tracking. No single subsystem can own this criterion in isolation; it must be owned at the NGHE Solution level, with explicit traceability to sub-FRs allocated to Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and Telemetry. The NGHE Solution module is responsible for ensuring that the integrated system achieves productivity_target while maintaining safety and operational compliance.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution is a high-level integration module with no direct implementation; it may become a 'black box' that obscures accountability. Mitigation: define explicit sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through FR-NGHE-001.5) and allocate each to a specific subsystem (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, Telemetry). Establish clear ownership and interface contracts.

Risk: Productivity measurement depends on accurate Telemetry data; if Telemetry is incomplete or unreliable, productivity_target cannot be verified. Mitigation: define Telemetry requirements (sampling rate, accuracy, latency) and establish data quality assurance procedures.

Risk: Safety Services constraints may conflict with productivity targets (e.g., overly conservative speed limits). Mitigation: involve Safety Manager in DP refinement to ensure safety constraints are necessary and proportionate, not arbitrary.

Risk: Network Link failures may prevent Teleoperation or Telemetry; if Teleoperation is unavailable, on-site operation must be sufficient to meet productivity_target. Mitigation: define fallback modes and ensure on-site operation is capable of sustaining productivity_target independently.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary for CR-NGHE-001. Recommended actions: (1) Establish explicit sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through FR-NGHE-001.5) and allocate to Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and Telemetry modules. (2) Define system-level interface contracts (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) with latency, accuracy, and availability requirements. (3) Establish a Productivity KPI dashboard (part of Telemetry) that tracks real-time and historical productivity metrics and alerts Site Manager to deviations from productivity_target. (4) Define acceptance test procedures that measure productivity under representative conditions and verify compliance with safety plan.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-C (productivity-centric architecture with Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and Telemetry modules) directly addresses the refined FR (enable operator to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity ≥ productivity_target while maintaining safety compliance). The DP is modular, separates concerns, and reduces coupling. The DP is implementable and testable. No fundamental mismatch between FR and DP.

diffusion_risks: Network Link is a cross-cutting concern shared by Teleoperation and Telemetry modules; network outages may affect both remote control and data streaming. Mitigation: define network redundancy strategy (e.g., dual connectivity, fallback to on-site operation).

Safety Services constraints are enforced across all modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Telemetry); changes to safety constraints may require updates to multiple modules. Mitigation: centralize safety constraint definitions in Safety Services module and provide a well-defined interface for other modules to query and enforce constraints.

Telemetry data collection is distributed across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services; data aggregation and quality assurance must be coordinated at the NGHE Solution level. Mitigation: define a Telemetry data schema and collection protocol that all modules follow.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: DP-C maps cleanly to the available IM modules: Vehicle (earthmoving tasks), Teleoperation (remote control), Safety Services (constraint enforcement), Telemetry (performance tracking). NGHE Solution is the integration layer that orchestrates these modules and owns the system-level productivity KPI. No new modules are required; the available modules are sufficient to realize DP-C.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: CR-NGHE-001 (Productivity KPI) is verifiable via representative operations trials: measure actual output (m³/h or ton/h) and compare against productivity_target. Telemetry module provides real-time and historical data for verification. Safety compliance is verifiable via incident/near-miss logs and safety audit checklist. Verification is feasible and does not require new infrastructure beyond what is already planned (Telemetry dashboard, safety monitoring).
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: CR-NGHE-001 is allocated to NGHE Solution with explicit sub-FRs allocated to Vehicle (FR-NGHE-001.1), on-site Operator (FR-NGHE-001.2), Tele-operator (FR-NGHE-001.3), Safety Services (FR-NGHE-001.4), and Telemetry (FR-NGHE-001.5). Allocation is clear and traceable. Each sub-FR is owned by a specific module or stakeholder, and interfaces between modules are well-defined (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link).

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-C to explicitly address the three coupling sources identified in the coupling_check: (1) define Teleoperation latency budget and safety override protocol; (2) specify Vehicle control responsiveness requirements; (3) establish Telemetry sampling strategy.
Define system-level interface contracts (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) with latency, accuracy, and availability requirements.
Establish a Productivity KPI dashboard (part of Telemetry) that tracks real-time and historical productivity metrics and alerts Site Manager to deviations from productivity_target.
Define acceptance test procedures that measure productivity under representative conditions and verify compliance with safety plan.
Involve Safety Manager in DP refinement to ensure safety constraints are necessary and proportionate, not arbitrary.
Define fallback modes for network outages and ensure on-site operation is capable of sustaining productivity_target independently.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level system acceptance criterion that requires decomposition into sub-FRs allocated to specific subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, Telemetry). The refined FR and selected DP are sound and pass the gate review, but further decomposition is necessary to allocate productivity responsibility to each subsystem and define the detailed requirements and interfaces for each module. Decomposition will also enable detailed design and implementation planning for each subsystem.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The Vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with mechanical capability sufficient to achieve productivity_target under nominal operating conditions.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The on-site Operator shall be able to command and monitor the Vehicle with sufficient situational awareness and control responsiveness to sustain productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The Tele-operator shall be able to command and monitor the Vehicle via Teleoperation with sufficient latency, fidelity, and safety interlocks to sustain productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The Safety Services shall continuously monitor operational state and enforce safety constraints (worksite plan, policies) without degrading productivity below acceptable threshold.
FR-NGHE-001.5: The NGHE Solution shall provide real-time Telemetry to Site Manager and Maintenance for productivity tracking, task logging, and predictive maintenance scheduling.

assumptions: Operator skill level (on-site and remote) is adequate to sustain productivity_target.
Worksite conditions (soil type, slope, obstacles) are within design envelope.
Network connectivity for Teleoperation is reliable (e.g., 4G/5G or private LTE).
Safety Services constraints do not conflict with productivity targets.
Telemetry infrastructure (sensors, data pipeline, dashboard) is available and maintained.
productivity_target is defined and agreed upon by customer and stakeholders.
Representative operations trials can be conducted under controlled conditions to verify productivity_target.
open_risks: Inadequate operator training or fatigue may degrade performance below target regardless of system capability.
Extreme worksite conditions may reduce achievable productivity below target.
Network outages may force fallback to on-site operation, reducing flexibility.
Overly conservative safety constraints may make productivity_target unachievable.
Sensor failures or data pipeline outages may blind Site Manager to productivity trends.
Coupling between Teleoperation latency and Safety Services constraint enforcement may create safety-critical timing issues.
Coupling between Vehicle control responsiveness and Teleoperation operator cycle-time targets may create performance bottlenecks.
Coupling between Telemetry data collection and real-time task execution may create bandwidth/CPU contention.
evidence_needed: Customer definition of productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) and evaluation period.
Worksite safety plan and operational policies that define safety constraints.
Vehicle specifications (hydraulic power, attachment types, control responsiveness) that support productivity_target.
Teleoperation latency budget and safety override protocol.
Telemetry sampling strategy and data quality assurance procedures.
Network connectivity specifications (bandwidth, latency, availability) for Teleoperation and Telemetry.
Acceptance test procedures and representative operations trial plan.
Safety Manager sign-off on safety constraints and risk mitigation measures.
